title: Kerberos in container can not be initiated
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

On my MacOS host system, I have built a container.
I have executed the container into bash. See step 1.
I was not able to initiate kerberos ticket by command `kinit phracek` into `IPA.REDHAT.COM`.
It failed with message in container:
```bash
kinit: Cannot find KDC for realm "IPA.REDHAT.COM" while getting initial credentials
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. run container
```bash
podman run -it  --rm -e WHOAMI=phracek -v /Users/phracek/etc_krb5/krb5.conf:/etc/krb5.conf:ro -v /Users/phracek/etc_krb5/krb5.conf.d:/etc/krb5.conf.d:ro quay.io/sclorg/cwt-devel bash
```

**Describe the results you received:**
```bash
kinit: Cannot find KDC for realm "IPA.REDHAT.COM" while getting initial credentials
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Kerberos ticket is active

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.1
API Version:  4.1.1
Go Version:   go1.18.3
Built:        Tue Jun 14 22:12:46 2022
OS/Arch:      darwin/amd64

Go Version:   go1.18.2
Built:        Mon May 30 18:03:28 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: I hit the same issue on Macbook M1 ARM version
Do you need to be on the VPN?  Is the VM connected to the VPN?

The host Mac OS is connected over LAN to RedHat network. No WiFi.

```bash
$ podman network ls
NETWORK ID    NAME        DRIVER
2f259bab93aa  podman      bridge
$ podman network inspect 2f259bab93aa

```
```
but the virtual machine doesn't have access.  Can you access IPA.REDHAT.COM from `podman machine ssh`?

Is there a way to mirror the connection to the virtual machine? I know it worked with the Docker Desktop, but unfortunately, I don't know what kind of magic they used to make it work.

I didn't have to do any configuration at all.

> but the virtual machine doesn't have access. Can you access IPA.REDHAT.COM from `podman machine ssh`?

`podman machine` does not have configured IPA.REDHAT.COM

```bash
$ cat /etc/krb5.conf
# To opt out of the system crypto-policies configuration of krb5, remove the
# symlink at /etc/krb5.conf.d/crypto-policies which will not be recreated.
includedir /etc/krb5.conf.d/

[logging]
    default = FILE:/var/log/krb5libs.log
    kdc = FILE:/var/log/krb5kdc.log
    admin_server = FILE:/var/log/kadmind.log

[libdefaults]
    dns_lookup_realm = false
    ticket_lifetime = 24h
    renew_lifetime = 7d
    forwardable = true
    rdns = false
    pkinit_anchors = FILE:/etc/pki/tls/certs/ca-bundle.crt
    spake_preauth_groups = edwards25519
    dns_canonicalize_hostname = fallback
    qualify_shortname = ""
#    default_realm = EXAMPLE.COM
    default_ccache_name = KEYRING:persistent:%{uid}

[realms]
# EXAMPLE.COM = {
#     kdc = kerberos.example.com
#     admin_server = kerberos.example.com
# }

[domain_realm]
# .example.com = EXAMPLE.COM
# example.com = EXAMPLE.COM
```

I have added an updated /etc/krb5.conf file with IPA.REDHAT.COM, but the situation has not changed.
The question is if I need to install to podman machine `kinit` and how to do it?

Could you volume mount it into the VM.

podman machine init -v /etc/krb5.conf:/etc/krb5.conf

Or do you need this in the container?

In case, I am calling init like this:
```bash
$ podman machine init
Extracting compressed file
Image resized.

$ podman machine start

require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

following command in your terminal session:

Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
```

But in case of:

```
What did I forget?

In the log you  can see differences between `podman machine start` and `podman machine start -v <krb>`
Does the kinit work when you volume mount in /etc/krb5.conf?

Nope, because I do not have an available socket.
```bash
Error: dial unix /var/folders/q5/l4tt4s1s1kxc0xd07_q7hhhh0000gn/T/podman/podman-machine-default_ready.sock: connect: connection refused
```
Can you podman machine ssh kinit?

Sorry:

```bash
$ podman machine ssh kinit
Warning: Permanently added '[localhost]:54300' (ED25519) to the list of known hosts.
bash: line 1: kinit: command not found
Error: exit status 127
```
How about `podman machine ssh podman run -v /etc/krb5.conf:/etc/krb5.conf IMAGEWITHKINIT kinit`

```bash
podman machine ssh podman run -v /Users/phracek/krb_ipa/krb5.conf:/etc/krb5.conf quay.io/sclorg/cwt-devel kinit
Warning: Permanently added '[localhost]:54300' (ED25519) to the list of known hosts.
kinit: Cannot find KDC for realm "IPA.REDHAT.COM" while getting initial credentials
Error: exit status 1
```

I will contact some kerberos folks, why I have `Cannot find KDC`
It looks like `kinit <krbname>@IPA.REDHAT.COM` does not work, but `kinit <krbname>@REDHAT.COM` works properly
Unfortunately, IPA.REDHAT.COM is needed for brew ;)
Could this be a rounting/vpn issue.  IE The networking in the VM does not see the IPA.REDHAT.COM host?

Although I am connected to VPN, it does not work.
By VM you mean `podman-machine-default`?

```bash
$ podman machine ls
NAME                     VM TYPE     CREATED      LAST UP            CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE
podman-machine-default*  qemu        4 hours ago  Currently running  1           2.147GB     107.4GB
```
In /etc/resolv.conf is not mentioned ipa.redhat.com:

```bash
$ podman machine ssh cat /etc/resolv.conf

nameserver 127.0.0.53
options edns0 trust-ad
search redhat.com win.redhat.com
```

Only for sure, on the host, in my case MacBook I am able to get kerberos ticket.

```bash
$ export KRB5CCNAME=FILE:/tmp/tgt2
$ kinit phracek
phracek@IPA.REDHAT.COM's password:
➜  tasks git:(master) klist
Credentials cache: FILE:/tmp/tgt2
        Principal: phracek@IPA.REDHAT.COM

  Issued                Expires               Principal
Jun 30 14:45:54 2022  Jul  1 00:45:48 2022  krbtgt/IPA.REDHAT.COM@IPA.REDHAT.COM
```

I have no idea, but this seems to have something to do with the internal networking of the VM not seeing the server for some reason.

You could try --network=host on your container to see if that works better.
I will try to use some RHEL image so I could get support ;)
I've seen that some "new" versions of dnsmasq causes problems with kerberos kdc, maybe a workaround like putting IPA.REDHAT.COM in podman machine /etc/hosts might work.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we have heard no further feedback, I am closing.  This also does not look like a Podman issue,

Hi, I've just tested the workaround and I don't think it's working properly.

The issue I have is with the dist-git authentication.

I've tried a simple "git pull" with a verbose output on one of my components and it cannot authenticate via KDC (take a look at the error):
```
debug1: Unspecified GSS failure.  Minor code may provide more information
Cannot find KDC for realm "REDHAT.COM"
```

My Kerberos ticket (I've tried it with two separate kerberos tickets):
1) `09/14/22 06:38:00  09/14/22 16:38:00  krbtgt/REDHAT.COM@REDHAT.COM`
2) `09/14/22 06:54:34  09/14/22 16:54:34  krbtgt/IPA.REDHAT.COM@IPA.REDHAT.COM`


What do you think about it?

I've tried to add `kdc = idm01.iam.prod.int.ams2.redhat.com` to the REDHAT.COM realm as well.

But then these errors occurred when tried the "git pull" on the component:
1) With `krbtgt/IPA.REDHAT.COM@IPA.REDHAT.COM`:
```
debug1: Unspecified GSS failure.  Minor code may provide more information
KDC returned error string: GET_LOCAL_TGT
```

debug1: Unspecified GSS failure.  Minor code may provide more information

Sorry, I have no clue about these errors.  I fail to see how they can be related to Podman though.

I've experienced the same issue. I think the issues had to do with how the container isn't able to use the DNS servers of the host, thus DNS resolution is failing.
`--network host` didn't do the trick either. The solution consisted in using the `--dns <RH DNS SERVER 1> --dns <RH DNS SERVER 2>` when creating the container. That solved the issue:
```
RB5_TRACE=/dev/stdout  kinit -t .kerberos.rmahroua.keytab -k rmahroua@IPA.REDHAT.COM
[222] 1688673372.443268: Matching rmahroua@IPA.REDHAT.COM in collection with result: 0/Success
[222] 1688673372.443269: Getting initial credentials for rmahroua@IPA.REDHAT.COM
[222] 1688673372.443270: Found entries for rmahroua@IPA.REDHAT.COM in keytab: aes256-cts
[222] 1688673372.443272: Sending unauthenticated request
[222] 1688673372.443273: Sending request (205 bytes) to IPA.REDHAT.COM
[222] 1688673372.443274: Sending DNS URI query for _kerberos.IPA.REDHAT.COM.
```

```
[rmahroua@toolbox ~]$ klist
Ticket cache: KEYRING:persistent:1000:1000
Default principal: rmahroua@IPA.REDHAT.COM

Valid starting     Expires            Service principal
07/06/23 19:56:23  07/07/23 05:56:23  krbtgt/IPA.REDHAT.COM@IPA.REDHAT.COM
	renew until 07/13/23 19:56:22
```
@Luap99 do have some network wizardry for the previous comment?

I think the issue likely is/was that the gvproxy resolver did not support all dns types. I think a srv record are needed for kerberos. That was however fixed a while ago https://github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock/pull/238 so I would assume that latest gvproxy does support it.

That said I know nothing about how kerberos actually works so maybe something else is going on.

@phracek could you test again?
If the issue still occurs, please re-open.
